title: galium cinereum subvar. pallidum
intro: Galium cinereum subvar. pallidum, also known as pale bedstraw, is a subvariety of the Galium cinereum plant, a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds and reducing fever. The health benefits of Galium cinereum subvar. pallidum include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
